nodes.name: RESTRICT + ROUTE (trust.js + router.js)
nodes.notes: SOURCE OF TRUTH: lib/f05/trust.js (scopeClaimsToApplication) + lib/f05/router.js (routeClaimTopic) -- do not edit here, edit there and re-paste.

nodes.name: CHECKS - dispatch (verifiers.js + entity_gate.js + quote_guard.js)
nodes.notes: SOURCE OF TRUTH: lib/f05/verifiers.js + lib/f05/entity_gate.js + lib/f05/quote_guard.js -- do not edit here, edit there and re-paste. Zero LLM, zero network call beyond the DB reads already done upstream.

parameters.content:
### ZERO LLM, ZERO EXTERNAL NETWORK CALL (design.md SS5.1)
gh_provenance + quote_guard + denominator extraction are all
deterministic. Any contradiction CANDIDATE they find still
passes the entity gate (steps 1-2 only, design SS6) before it
may become a `contradicts` row -- but the LLM hook (step 3) is
NEVER called from this workflow. That call lives only in
f05-contradiction-scan, matching lib/f05/run.js's own explicit
choice ('step 3 is omitted, owned by C1b').

### THE LLM CONTRADICTION QUEUE IS NOT THREADED THROUGH HERE
design.md SS11.1 describes 'the narrow queue f05-verify-claims
routes to' f05-contradiction-scan. This generator keeps the two
workflows independently callable/testable instead (matching
f05-trust-rollup's own precedent of being a separate,
independently-invoked sub-workflow rather than an automatic
continuation) -- f05-contradiction-scan builds its OWN narrow
queue from { application_id } by the identical eligibility rule
(self_reported / deck-sourced claims). Open decision, documented
rather than silently made -- see n8n/workflows/README-f05.md.

nodes.name: Note: narrow queue lives in contradiction-scan
